title: Gastrochilus pusillus
intro: Gastrochilus pusillus, also known as the small-mouthed orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Gastrochilus pusillus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into various herbal preparations, such as capsules and ointments.
taxonomy: Gastrochilus pusillus is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Within the Orchidaceae family, Gastrochilus pusillus is classified as a genus of orchids. The genus Gastrochilus is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification places Gastrochilus pusillus within the plant kingdom's taxonomic hierarchy.
